topic: Why people obey authority even when they should not — Milgram

script.title: Why You'll Obey a Voice You Shouldn't

scenes.narration: You like to think you'd refuse. That if someone in a lab coat told you to hurt a stranger, you'd walk out. You wouldn't.
scenes.image_prompt: Empty wooden chair under a single harsh overhead lamp, black and white film noir, long shadows stretching across a bare concrete floor, clinical and cold

scenes.narration: In 1961, Stanley Milgram ran an experiment that destroyed what we believed about ourselves. Ordinary people. A fake shock machine. And one man in a gray coat.
scenes.image_prompt: Vintage control panel with rows of unlabeled switches, dim tungsten glow, heavy shadows, black and white film noir, dust motes in the air

scenes.narration: Sixty-five percent of subjects delivered what they believed were lethal shocks. Not sociopaths. Teachers. Accountants. Your neighbors. Your father.
scenes.image_prompt: A single hand gripping an industrial lever, knuckles white, tight crop, deep shadows, black and white film noir, grainy texture

scenes.narration: They screamed. They sweated. They begged the experimenter to stop. And then they kept pressing the button anyway.
scenes.image_prompt: Clenched fist resting on a metal table beside a small red button, sweat beads visible, black and white film noir, harsh side lighting

scenes.narration: Milgram discovered something darker than cruelty. You don't need to be evil to do evil. You only need someone above you who tells you it's fine.
scenes.image_prompt: A stark empty staircase ascending into darkness, iron railings casting bar-like shadows on the wall, black and white film noir, oppressive angle

scenes.narration: Your brain has an off switch. It's called the agentic state. Once you hand responsibility to an authority, your conscience goes quiet.
scenes.image_prompt: An old telephone receiver hanging off the hook, cord coiled like a noose, dust on the cradle, black and white film noir, silent room

scenes.narration: This is why uniforms work. Why clipboards work. Why a confident voice on the phone can drain your bank account by dinner.
scenes.image_prompt: A clipboard and pen resting on a wooden desk beside a worn leather briefcase, heavy shadow falling across the page, black and white film noir

scenes.narration: You are not the exception. You are the rule. The next time someone above you tells you to cross a line, remember — the button was always in your hand.
scenes.image_prompt: A cracked mirror reflecting a single red button on a gray surface, jagged glass fractures, black and white film noir, low angle, heavy vignette
